cityDNA.coreFantasy: A colossal floodplain civilization where lock houses, grain halls, relief plazas, healing courts, training arenas, memory-tablet government, and menhir courts make scale responsible.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Ghorum Vale is not a generic giant city; its grandeur is logistical, communal, and memorial.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Ghorum Vale turns water control, food, relief, healing, training, public rosters, memory tablets, and menhir-scale remembrance into one floodplain system of civic continuity.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: White limestone civic blocks, reed pavilions, coral terraces, market arcades, timber longhouses, lock houses, waterwheel courts, grain halls, relief plazas, healing wards, training grounds, memory tablets, and colossal menhirs.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A colossus-scale civilization where large bodies and large systems create public responsibility around water, food, relief, care, readiness, and remembered duty.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Lock houses, waterwheels, canal reflections, grain halls, awnings, blue lamps, memory tablets, menhirs, woven banners, champion gates, and healing lanterns symbolize burden-sharing at scale.

canonLocks: Ghorum Vale is a mature colossus-scale floodplain capital, not a generic giant city.
The Wonder is the Colossal Floodplain Menhir Court.
The Beacon is the Lock House Beacon.
The Seat of Power is the Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry.
The five primary districts are Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Civic Services, Medical / Healing, and Arena / Training.

The uploaded district anchors include Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Lock House Beacon, Canal Reflections Monument, Waterwheel Court Nook, Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Grain Hall, Awning Monument, Mill Court Nook, Civic Services Hall, Relief Hall, Civic Services Lantern Monument, Notice Plaza Nook, Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Healing Hall, Medical Healing Lantern Monument, Apothecary Court Nook, Arena / Training Civic Hall, Training Arena, Woven Banners Monument, and Champion Gate Nook.
provisionalCanon: Ghorum Vale's highest civic virtue is continuity.
The central tension is public care versus impersonal administration.
Scale should express obligation: huge buildings matter only because they manage water, food, aid, healing, training, and memory.
rejectedReads: Ghorum Vale is merely oversized architecture.
Ghorum Vale is interchangeable with other giant or troll cities.
Food, relief, healing, and training are background services rather than primary civic institutions.
cautionFlags: Use scale through functions: water gauges, grain scales, basins, doors, stairs, wheels, tablets, and plazas built for colossal civic life.
Keep water, food, relief, healing, and training visible across the city.
Memory must appear in public stone, tablets, rosters, and menhir alignments, not only private nostalgia.
Preserve exact sidecar anchors so Ghorum Vale does not become a generic big floodplain city.

environment.environmentalLogic: Floodplain water is the basis of government, labor, danger, food, memory, and public care. The city measures, gates, reflects, stores, and routes water rather than pretending it can be ignored.

skyline: Colossal Floodplain Menhir Court as far wonder anchor
Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry as central authority
Lock House Beacon as riverworks signal landmark
Training Arena as strength and readiness anchor
grain halls, healing halls, relief towers, waterworks, and canal bridges across the floodplain basin

civilization.people: Citizens are large-bodied, procedural, communal, and accustomed to public burdens. Strength matters when it keeps water, food, relief, healing, and readiness systems working.
civilization.governance: Authority is logistical-memory based. The Registry governs by remembering obligations, managing water, feeding people, providing relief, healing the injured, training replacements, and assigning public burdens.
civilization.religion: Religious expression is memorial and floodplain-rooted, carried through menhir courts, water reflection rites, healing lanterns, woven training banners, burden-taking vows, and public remembrance.

streets.appearance: Enormous, wet, busy, and procedural: towpaths, broad canal bridges, lock-house plazas, grain halls, relief queues, healing gardens, arena approaches, and memory-tablet courts built for colossal bodies and burdens.
streets.movement: Movement follows towpaths, canal bridges, lock roads, waterwheel yards, market arcades, broad civic stairs, relief plazas, healing courts, arena approaches, and boat routes through managed channels.
streets.lighting: Daylight reflects from canals, white civic blocks, wet stone, reed roofs, and food-market awnings; night is blue-marked and lantern-active across locks, healing halls, and canal reflections.
streets.weatherEffects: Rain, haze, river fog, humidity, and rising water change routes, crowd behavior, relief demand, and civic urgency.
streets.dayCycle: Morning begins with lock readings, waterwheel checks, grain halls opening, relief notices posted, healing rounds, and training drills.
streets.nightCycle: Night settles into lock-house lamps, healing lanterns, market windows, menhir shadows, blue service lights, and canal reflections.

housing.wealthy: Large white-stone registry or waterworks houses with raised flood-safe foundations, broad thresholds, private basins, grain storage, tablet alcoves, and canal views.
housing.middle: Timber and limestone family homes near mills, markets, healing halls, or towpaths, with storage lofts, drying racks, shared basins, and heavy benches.
housing.poor: Raised floodplain dwellings, reed-and-stone rooms, shared courtyards, water marks, sealed jars, repair kits, and close dependence on relief halls or grain markets.

districts.name: Canal / Riverworks
districts.civicPurpose: Hydraulic coordination, flood warning, water routing, lock operation, and the city's public signal heart.
districts.visualIdentity: Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Lock House Beacon, Canal Reflections Monument, Waterwheel Court Nook, wet stone, sluice gates, towpaths, and broad canal bridges.

districts.name: Granary / Food Market
districts.civicPurpose: Public food security, market logistics, grain storage, bread production, and visible provision.
districts.visualIdentity: Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Grain Hall, Awning Monument, Mill Court Nook, covered food markets, reed pavilions, grain scales, bread ovens, and storage jars.

districts.name: Civic Services
districts.civicPurpose: Organized public aid, relief eligibility, rosters, notices, and administrative compassion.
districts.visualIdentity: Civic Services Hall, Relief Hall, Civic Services Lantern Monument, Notice Plaza Nook, public rosters, queues, water basins, and blue service lamps.

districts.name: Medical / Healing
districts.civicPurpose: Recovery, triage, public health, healing supplies, apothecary work, and care for injured workers.
districts.visualIdentity: Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Healing Hall, Medical Healing Lantern Monument, Apothecary Court Nook, white civic blocks, gardens, and water bowls.

districts.name: Arena / Training
districts.civicPurpose: Disciplined readiness, replacement training, champion culture, and strength formed into civic responsibility.
districts.visualIdentity: Arena / Training Civic Hall, Training Arena, Woven Banners Monument, Champion Gate Nook, lecture courts, practice rings, arena terraces, and public tests.

wonder.name: Colossal Floodplain Menhir Court
wonder.symbolicRole: Memory made physically massive, communal, floodplain-rooted, and durable enough to outlast seasonal danger.
wonder.visualRole: Vast menhir court of monumental stones, floodplain terraces, civic approaches, water reflections, carved memory surfaces, and colossal ritual scale.
wonder.civicRole: Wonder-scale site for flood remembrance rites, public registry ceremonies, burden-taking vows, harvest acknowledgments, training graduations, and citywide mourning.
storyHooks: A menhir inscription forces the city to remember a duty its rosters erased.

beacon.name: Lock House Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Water control, flood warning, and emergency coordination made public signal.
beacon.visualRole: Major beacon among lock houses, wet stone, waterwheel courts, white limestone blocks, reed pavilions, canal reflections, and towpath traffic.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates flood warnings, lock-house musters, sluice gate orders, waterwheel shutdowns, public announcements, grain halls, relief rosters, healing teams, trainers, and registry clerks.
storyHooks: Control of the Lock House Beacon controls the city's emergency tempo.

seatOfPower.name: Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Public memory, duty records, sealed archives, command terraces, and colossus-scale civic continuity made government.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Central registry complex of memory tablet walls, white stone courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, blue lamps, and large public approach stairs.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Government center for registration, roster sealing, water-right hearings, relief reviews, public petitions, training rulings, and memory ceremonies.
storyHooks: A roster category determines whether a real person receives relief or disappears into administration.

majorLandmarks.name: Grain Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Huge grain hall with scales, bread ovens, awnings, storage jars, reed pavilions, and market traffic.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Stores grain, organizes food markets, weighs supplies, and supports public provision.
storyHooks: A grain tally proves the city has enough food but not enough honesty.

majorLandmarks.name: Relief Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Relief hall with blue lanterns, notice plaza, public rosters, queues, water basins, and civic service counters.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Aid eligibility, public rosters, emergency relief, notices, and response coordination.
storyHooks: A relief notice names someone who should not be alive.

majorLandmarks.name: Healing Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Healing hall with lantern monument, apothecary court, herb stalls, white civic blocks, water bowls, and recovery beds.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Medical care, triage, public health, and injured-worker recovery.
storyHooks: A recovery list exposes which workers the city considers replaceable.

majorLandmarks.name: Training Arena
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Arena with woven banners, champion gate, practice rings, broad terraces, students, and public tests.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Training, readiness, replacement preparation, champion recognition, and public discipline.
storyHooks: A champion gate ceremony decides who carries the next public burden.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Lock House Beacon, Canal Reflections Monument, Waterwheel Court Nook, sluice gates, lock workers, and broad canal bridges.
S2 must show Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Grain Hall, Awning Monument, Mill Court Nook, grain scales, bread ovens, and market crowds.
S3 must show Civic Services Hall, Relief Hall, Civic Services Lantern Monument, Notice Plaza Nook, public rosters, relief clerks, and queues.
S4 must show Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Healing Hall, Medical Healing Lantern Monument, Apothecary Court Nook, healers, herbs, and recovery beds.
S5 must show Arena / Training Civic Hall, Training Arena, Woven Banners Monument, Champion Gate Nook, practice rings, arena trainers, and students.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Show a colossal floodplain city organized around lock houses, grain halls, relief plazas, healing courts, training arenas, memory-tablet government, and menhir processional grounds.

The Colossal Floodplain Menhir Court should be the far wonder anchor; the Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry should be central authority; the Lock House Beacon should tie the city to floodplain water control.
elementsToAvoid: food and relief hidden from view
